decisions.summary: Default-off opt-in. merge.autoMerge.enabled defaults to false in both global ~/.panopticon/config.yaml and per-project .pan.yaml. The 'humans only merge' safety rule is preserved unless the operator explicitly opts in.

Auto-merge uses the same triggerMerge() code path the MERGE button uses (src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts ~line 4549). No alternate merge path — the manual button and the auto-merge scheduler converge on the same function, so all queue serialization, post-merge lifecycle, Docker cleanup, and idempotency guards apply unchanged.

Trigger source = the existing readyForMerge false→true transition in src/lib/review-status.ts (~line 373). The scheduler hooks into this transition rather than polling. Boot recovery re-scans the DB for pending auto-merges with future executeAt and re-arms timers.

Cooldown state persisted in SQLite (new auto_merge table: issue_id, scheduled_at, execute_at, status enum, cancel_reason). Survives dashboard restart. In-memory Map<issueId, timeoutHandle> is the live arm; DB row is the source of truth.

Default cooldown = 5 minutes, configurable via merge.autoMerge.cooldownMinutes. Minimum 1 minute (enforced clamp — values below 1 are coerced up). Wall-clock based on executeAt timestamp — host sleep does NOT reset the timer; it just delays the fire and gates are re-validated at fire time.

Gate re-validation at execution time. Right before calling triggerMerge, the scheduler re-checks: readyForMerge==true, no blocker labels, all configured commit status checks green, GitHub CI passing (if requireGitHubCiPassing). If any gate flipped during cooldown, emit merge.auto.aborted with reason and do NOT merge. The cooldown is a window for the operator and for the world to push new state, not a fire-and-forget timer.
Default blocker label set: ['needs-design', 'needs-discussion', 'do-not-merge', 'wip']. Operator can override via merge.autoMerge.requireNoBlockerLabels. Empty list means 'no blocker labels checked'.

Stale-fire protection. If executeAt is more than 60 minutes in the past at boot or wake (e.g. laptop was asleep for hours), the scheduler emits merge.auto.aborted with reason='stale' and does NOT auto-execute. Operator must re-trigger manually. Window is configurable as merge.autoMerge.maxStaleMinutes (default 60).

Multi-PR queue uses the existing merge-queue-db serializer (src/lib/database/merge-queue-db.ts). The scheduler always calls triggerMerge which enqueues if another merge is in flight. No separate auto-merge queue; concurrent scheduled auto-merges naturally serialize via the existing queue at execution time.

TTS strategy: emit one priority=1 announcement at schedule ('PAN-XXXX auto-merging in N minutes — say pan merge cancel PAN-XXXX to abort'); emit one priority=2 announcement at T-30s if cooldownMinutes > 1; emit one priority=1 announcement on execute, cancel, or abort. No periodic spam — at most 3 utterances per cooldown.

Cancel surface: POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel + `pan merge cancel <id>` CLI command. Cancel only succeeds during the pending window. Once the scheduler has called triggerMerge, cancel returns 409 — recovery is via the existing merge-failure recovery path. Cancel can be called when there is no pending auto-merge; in that case returns 200 with cancelled=false (idempotent).

Event store events emitted: merge.auto.scheduled (payload: issueId, executeAt, cooldownSeconds), merge.auto.cancelled (payload: issueId, reason, cancelledBy), merge.auto.executed (payload: issueId), merge.auto.aborted (payload: issueId, gateFailureReason). The dashboard banner subscribes to these via the existing /ws/rpc subscribeDomainEvents stream.

Dashboard banner forks the GraceCountdown.tsx pattern (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/GraceCountdown.tsx). New file: MergeAutoMergeCountdown.tsx. Renders inline above the MergeButton zone on issue cards and in the inspector. Cancel button → POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel.

Read-model extension: add autoMergeScheduled: { executeAt: string; scheduledAt: string } | null to the issue read-model. Populated from DB on snapshot bootstrap and from merge.auto.* events on the wire. Dashboard banner gates on this field.

Logging discipline. Every state transition logs to console with [merge-auto] prefix and the issueId: scheduled, cancelled, aborted (with reason), executed, failed. This is the acceptance criterion 'Logged loudly on every step'.

CLI surface follows the Skills <-> CLI convention (docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md). The new `pan merge` verb (with the `cancel` subcommand) gets a wrapper skill at sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md. scripts/lint-skills.sh must accept the new verb. CLI dispatch goes through src/cli/index.ts following the approveCommand pattern.

hazards.summary: Race: a merge fires while the scheduler is still in cooldown. If the operator clicks MERGE manually during the cooldown window, two calls to triggerMerge could race.
hazards.mitigation: triggerMerge already has the merge-queue serializer at src/lib/database/merge-queue-db.ts that atomically marks an issue as 'processing'. Second caller will be enqueued or no-op. The scheduler should also check 'am I still pending in the DB' immediately before firing and bail if state is no longer 'pending' (operator-merge happened first).

hazards.summary: Idempotency loop: post-merge lifecycle calling back into review-status updates could flip readyForMerge and re-trigger the scheduler. CLAUDE.md cites PAN-328 — a similar loop burned 24,626 Linear API calls.
hazards.mitigation: Scheduler MUST only schedule on a true false→true transition where mergeStatus is null/pending — never schedule when mergeStatus is merged/merging/queued/failed. Also: when scheduling, check there is no existing pending auto_merge row for this issue and bail if there is. Add unit tests covering the loop scenarios.

hazards.summary: Blocker label race: a label is added during cooldown but the GitHub webhook hasn't fired yet, so the local view of labels is stale.
hazards.mitigation: Gate re-validation at execute time queries GitHub directly (gh pr view --json labels) rather than trusting local cache, when requireNoBlockerLabels is non-empty. Fail closed: if the label query fails or times out (>5s), abort with reason='label-check-timeout' rather than firing optimistically.

hazards.summary: Test-suite interference: existing test code that exercises review-status transitions could trigger the auto-merge scheduler during tests, leading to spurious merge calls or flaky tests.
hazards.mitigation: The scheduler is initialized only when the dashboard server boots (not at module import). Tests that import review-status modules will not start the scheduler. Provide a test-only scheduler with injectable clock and disable-on-import semantics (PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE=1 env var, default in test environment).

hazards.summary: Recursive feature risk: this PR is itself a Panopticon work item. If the operator opts in to auto-merge before testing this feature, the PR for PAN-1418 could auto-merge itself before any human reviews the auto-merge behavior.
hazards.mitigation: Default merge.autoMerge.enabled is false. Acceptance criteria require explicit operator opt-in. Add a startup log at dashboard boot when auto-merge is enabled — 'AUTO-MERGE ENABLED for project <key>' — so it is unmissable. Document that this feature should be enabled only after the operator has watched at least one full auto-merge cycle on a low-risk issue.

hazards.summary: Cancel TOCTOU: cancel arrives just as the scheduler is firing. Could leave the DB row marked 'cancelled' while the merge proceeds.
hazards.mitigation: Use a single DB transaction in the scheduler's fire path: SELECT row WHERE status='pending', UPDATE to status='executing' returning affected rows; if 0 rows updated (cancel won the race), do NOT call triggerMerge. The cancel endpoint UPDATE WHERE status='pending' is similarly atomic — if 0 rows updated, the cancel arrived after the fire began.

hazards.summary: Operator confusion if the banner shows a countdown but the host is offline (e.g. mobile dashboard viewing a remote host that crashed). The countdown ticks visually but no merge will happen.
hazards.mitigation: The banner subscribes to merge.auto.executed/aborted/cancelled events. If the WebSocket disconnects, the banner shows a 'connection lost' indicator and stops claiming the merge will happen. On reconnect, it re-reads the snapshot.

hazards.summary: GitHub Enterprise / GitLab compatibility: the requireGitHubCiPassing gate assumes GitHub. Mixed-forge projects (GitHub + GitLab via the forge adapter) may break.
hazards.mitigation: Implement gate check through the existing src/lib/forge.ts getForgeAdapter abstraction so GitLab uses its own CI status endpoint. Document that the feature is only validated on GitHub for v1; emit a config validation warning if requireGitHubCiPassing is set on a project that doesn't have a GitHub forge configured.

hazards.summary: No Linear blocker labels concept. requireNoBlockerLabels assumes GitHub Issues. Linear-tracked issues would skip the label gate.
hazards.mitigation: Gate check reads forge labels only when forge is GitHub-like. For other trackers (Linear), the label gate is a no-op and a comment in the schema documents that. Future expansion can add Linear state-name checks behind a separate flag.

hazards.summary: Single-developer assumption: this is explicitly for solo workflows. If multiple developers share a Panopticon instance and one of them opts in via .pan.yaml, the other dev's PRs would also auto-merge.
hazards.mitigation: Document this clearly in the configuration reference (CLAUDE.md and the new auto-merge.mdx). Add an acceptance criterion that the config docs explicitly say 'solo workflows only — do not enable in shared/team Panopticon instances'.

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1418: Optional opt-in auto-merge for single-developer workflow (configurable per-project)

Auto planning completed. Mapped merge flow (triggerMerge at workspaces.ts:4549, readyForMerge transition at review-status.ts:373, queue at merge-queue-db.ts, TTS at activity-logger.ts:123, GraceCountdown template at GraceCountdown.tsx). Wrote 16 decisions + 10 hazards, 17 beads with one foundation chain (config schema -> DB schema -> scheduler service) marked requiresInspection=deep.
